gold_answer: The short title is the Customs Act 1960.
Customs duty is imposed on dutiable goods according to the Act and relevant schedules.
A key function is administering and enforcing customs laws, including supervision of import and export controls.
It is the Central Provident Fund Act 1953.
The scheme provides mandatory savings for retirement and certain housing and healthcare needs.
Employers and eligible employees are generally required to make CPF contributions in accordance with the Act.
CareShield Life provides long-term care insurance payouts for severe disability.
Payouts typically begin when the insured person meets the severe disability criteria defined under the scheme.
Non-compliance may result in penalties such as fines, seizure, or prosecution under the Act.
They are Singapore statutes governing customs, compulsory savings, and long-term care insurance respectively.
